concept: Empty / Containing Nothing / Void of Contents
description: Containing nothing; not filled or occupied. 'Muloe' names the quality of emptiness—space that holds no contents, vessel ready to receive.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to nothing. 'Mu' begins with closed potential, space present. 'Lo' moves through vacancy, nothing encountered. Finally 'e' opens to pure space, emptiness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'muloe noru' (empty bowl). Opposite of 'pheno' (full).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Emptiness (sunyata) is not nothingness but openness—empty of fixed self, full of possibility.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Empty space shaped design—negative space as essential as positive form.
